name: Young-Donor Mitochondrial Transfusion
summary: Preclinical transfer or infusion of mitochondria isolated from young donors into aged animals or aged tissues.
mechanism_summary: The intervention concept attempts to supplement aged or stressed tissues with mitochondria from young donors, with proposed effects on ATP production, oxidative stress, mitochondrial-complex abundance, apoptosis, and tissue bioenergetics.

open_questions: Whether young-donor mitochondrial delivery can be safely translated outside animal models
Whether functional improvements persist beyond short experimental windows
Which tissues receive, retain, and functionally integrate transferred mitochondria after systemic delivery
Whether allogeneic or engineered sources can avoid immune and mtDNA-compatibility concerns
